# Documentation Index

Welcome to the documentation for David Auble's personal website! This index will help you find the information you need.

## 📚 Documentation Structure

### Getting Started

Perfect for new users and contributors:

- **[README.md](../README.md)** - Start here! Project overview, installation, and basic usage
- **[QUICKSTART.md](../QUICKSTART.md)** - Get up and running in 5 minutes
- **[FAQ.md](FAQ.md)** - Answers to common questions

### Development

For developers working on the project:

- **[DEVELOPMENT.md](../DEVELOPMENT.md)** - Detailed development guide, Jekyll configuration, and customization
- **[ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)** - System architecture and technical overview
- **[CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)** - How to contribute, code style, and PR process

### Operations

For deployment and maintenance:

- **[DEPLOYMENT.md](DEPLOYMENT.md)** - Deployment process, monitoring, and troubleshooting
- **[SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md)** - Security policy and vulnerability reporting
- **[CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md)** - Project version history and changes

### Community

Guidelines for participation:

- **[CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)** - Community standards and behavior expectations

## 🎯 Quick Navigation

### I want to...

#### ...understand what this project is
→ Read [README.md](../README.md)

#### ...set up the project locally
→ Follow [QUICKSTART.md](../QUICKSTART.md)

#### ...add a blog post
→ See [DEVELOPMENT.md](../DEVELOPMENT.md#content-management)

#### ...customize the design
→ Check [DEVELOPMENT.md](../DEVELOPMENT.md#theme-and-styling)

#### ...contribute to the project
→ Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)

#### ...report a bug or security issue
→ See [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md) for security, GitHub Issues for bugs

#### ...understand how deployment works
→ Read [DEPLOYMENT.md](DEPLOYMENT.md)

#### ...learn about the architecture
→ Review [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)

#### ...find answers to common questions
→ Browse [FAQ.md](FAQ.md)

#### ...see what's changed recently
→ Check [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md)

## 📖 Documentation by Role

### For End Users

If you're browsing the website or reading blog posts:
- The live site: https://davidauble.com
- Blog posts are in the `_posts/` directory (if reading source)

### For Contributors

If you're contributing code or content:
1. Start with [QUICKSTART.md](../QUICKSTART.md)
2. Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
3. Review [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
4. Refer to [DEVELOPMENT.md](../DEVELOPMENT.md) as needed

### For Maintainers

If you're maintaining the project:
1. Understand the [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)
2. Know the [DEPLOYMENT.md](DEPLOYMENT.md) process
3. Keep [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md) procedures current
4. Update [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md) regularly

### For System Administrators

If you're managing infrastructure:
1. Review [DEPLOYMENT.md](DEPLOYMENT.md)
2. Understand [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)
3. Monitor per [DEPLOYMENT.md](DEPLOYMENT.md#monitoring--logging)

## 🔍 Documentation Standards

All documentation in this project follows these standards:

### Markdown Format
- GitHub Flavored Markdown
- Clear headings and structure
- Code blocks with language specification
- Tables for structured data

### Writing Style
- Clear and concise
- Active voice
- Step-by-step instructions where applicable
- Examples for complex concepts

### Updates
- Keep documentation in sync with code
- Update CHANGELOG.md for notable changes
- Review quarterly for accuracy

## 📝 Contributing to Documentation

Documentation improvements are always welcome! To contribute:

1. **Small fixes** (typos, clarity): Submit a PR directly
2. **Large changes**: Open an issue first to discuss
3. **New documentation**: Propose structure in an issue

Follow the same process as code contributions (see [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)).

## 🆘 Need Help?

If you can't find what you're looking for:

1. **Search**: Use GitHub's search to find relevant info
2. **FAQ**: Check [FAQ.md](FAQ.md) for common questions
3. **Issues**: Search existing issues on GitHub
4. **Ask**: Open a new issue with the "question" label

## 📂 File Structure Reference

```
dauble.github.io/
├── README.md              # Project overview and setup
├── QUICKSTART.md          # Quick setup guide
├── CONTRIBUTING.md        # Contribution guidelines
├── CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md     # Community standards
├── SECURITY.md            # Security policy
├── CHANGELOG.md           # Version history
├── DEVELOPMENT.md         # Development guide
├── docs/                  # Additional documentation
│   ├── README.md          # This file
│   ├── ARCHITECTURE.md    # Architecture overview
│   ├── DEPLOYMENT.md      # Deployment guide
│   └── FAQ.md             # Frequently asked questions
├── _posts/                # Blog post content
├── _layouts/              # Page templates
├── _includes/             # Reusable components
├── _sass/                 # SCSS stylesheets
├── assets/                # Images, scripts, fonts
└── _config.yml            # Jekyll configuration
```
